question: [File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: formatScheduleRow() / utils.nsToSecNs()] Can an unprivileged external user cause an unhandled exception in formatScheduleRow() by requesting a schedule whose consensus_timestamp, executed_timestamp, or expiration_time fields contain extreme or null values that cause utils.nsToSecNs() to throw a TypeError or return an unexpected type, crashing the handler and, at scale, the API?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getSchedules() / Promise.all()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a partial failure cascade by sending requests where the entityQuery succeeds but the signatureQuery fails (or vice versa), causing Promise.all() to reject and throw a DbError that, if not properly handled by the error middleware, leaves the response in an inconsistent state and crashes the handler?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DB index bypass by supplying a schedule.id filter with a `gte` or `lte` operator combined with a very small or very large schedule ID value, causing the schedulesQuery to scan a large portion of the schedule table index and, at high concurrency, saturate DB I/O and prevent transaction confirmation?
